hero.eyebrow: A Hale Family Restaurant · Salt Lake City & Midvale
hero.headline: Just Pizza.
hero.body: Family-owned since 1965. Same crust. Same sauce. Same gold lettering on the box. Walk in. Order a Classic. Pour yourself a Hires. Sit down.
story.headline: Don loaded up the kids
story.p1: In 1965, Don Hale already had a hamburger drive-in on his hands — Hires Big H — six years deep and the busiest joint in Salt Lake. He didn't need another restaurant. He just loved pizza.
story.p2: So he piled the family into the car and drove. Utah. Idaho. Wyoming. Colorado. Anywhere he heard there was a good slice. He tasted, he asked questions, he wrote things down. The kids loved it.
story.p3: When he came home he started building. Sauce. Dough. Garlic bread. Salad dressing. Spaghetti. He wanted a name that fit. Something with a z, because a name with a z sounds solid.
story.p4: Litzas. Rhymes with pizza. Sounds like it means something. Sixty years later, it does.
menu.headline: Pizzas named after Utah peaks.
menu.body: The full menu is below. Photos are owner-review stand-ins until the camera comes through. The pizza is the same as it has always been.
bridge.headline: Same Family.
bridge.p1: Hires Big H came first — Don Hale opened it in 1959. Litzas came six years later. In Midvale, we share a building. In Salt Lake, we share a parking lot. Different menus, one family, one set of values.
bridge.p2: If you grew up with a frosty mug of Hires Root Beer, you already know how this story goes.
loc.headline: Two shops.
loc.body: Salt Lake City and Midvale. Walk in. Call ahead. We'll have a pie waiting.
